translations.descricao: Anxiety disorders represent a group of common psychiatric conditions, characterized by excessive worry and fear, impacting daily functionality. Early screening in primary care is crucial to identify cases and initiate interventions, reducing the risk of chronicity. In the Brazilian context, integration into the SUS promotes equitable access to diagnosis.
translations.sus.indicacao: Screening indicated for individuals with persistent anxiety symptoms in basic care, including adults and adolescents with emotional complaints.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Adults ≥18 years with risk factors such as chronic stress or comorbidities; adolescents with signs of psychological distress.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The American Psychiatric Association (2023) recommends systematic screening in adults with anxiety symptoms in primary care settings.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: The global prevalence of anxiety disorders is approximately 4% in adults. In Brazil, it is estimated at 9.3% of the adult population.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The annual incidence varies from 2-5% in adult populations. In the Brazilian context, about 3% new cases per year.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Low direct mortality, but associated with a 20-30% increased risk of suicide. In Brazil, it indirectly contributes to 5% of deaths from external causes.
